messages.content:
When, on March 11, 1941, the Lend-Lease Act first became law, Britain stood virtually alone before the tide of Axis aggression which had swept across Western Europe. Everywhere the peace-loving peoples of the world were facing disaster. But the passage of the Lend-Lease Act gave firm assurance to those resisting the aggressors that the overpowering material resources of the United States were on their side.
- Source: Univelsity of California at Santa Barbara
Which factor contributed to the extension of the act discussed in this excerpt?
• Allied nations needed supplies to continue the war effort against Nazi Germany.
• Neutrality prevented Americans from boarding ships belonging to nations at war.
O Reparation payments from former Allied nations were suspended by executive order.
The Kellogg-Briand Pact called for participants to refrain from warfare to resolve conflicts.
Allied nations needed supplies to continue the war effort against Nazi Germany.

we here highly resole that these dead shall not have died in vain
REMBER DEC 7th! Which event did this poster commemorate?
the battle of Iwo Jima
the surrender at Bataan
the attack on Pearl Harbor
O
the invasion at Normandy Beach
The poster commemorates the attack on Pearl Harbor.

Study the following timeline, which shows key accomplishments of the Allies in Europe during World War II, then answer the question below
1
2
3
4
Invasion of
Normandy
Liberation of Paris
A
Crossing of the Rhine
Which number on the timeline shows when the Battle of the Bulge occurred? Which number on the timeline shows when the Battle of the Bulge occurred?
1
2
3 or 4

The Battle of the Bulge occurred after the crossing of the Rhine and it is not included in this timeline. Therefore, the number on the timeline that shows when the Battle of the Bulge occurred is not present.

From Frederick Turner's 1893 thesis, "The Significance of the Frontier in American History"
• American development is linked with the need to move and expand westward.
• Once the western frontier is settled, Americans will need new territories for continued development.
Source: Library of Congress
Which U.S. government action resulted from these ideas?
AR Tools
the declaration of the Roosevelt Corollary
United States participation in the Boxer Rebellion United States involvement in the Spanish-American War
the negotiation of the Gentlemen's Agreement with Japan

The notion that Americans would need new territories for continued development as stated in Frederick Turner's thesis contributed to United States involvement in the Spanish-American War, which resulted in the acquisition of new territories such as Puerto Rico, the Philippines, and Guam.

How did the rise of big business influence U.S. foreign policy in the late nineteenth century?
The government created a training program for diplomats sent to other countries.
• The need for inexpensive raw materials justified the acquisition of new territories.
• The rise of industrial monopolies encouraged the passage of immigration restrictions.
• The government adopted a strategy of isolationism to protect industries from competition.
The need for inexpensive raw materials justified the acquisition of new territories.

USH_U3_FSQ1_FY23

Which generalization can be made about the United States and the number of battleships?
• The United States had seen an increase in joint naval operations with allied nations.
• The United States had a desire to improve international communications.
O The United States had acquired numerous overseas territories.
• The United States had a desire to protect merchant vessels.
The generalization that can be made about the United States and the number of battleships is that the United States had a desire to protect merchant vessels, which required a strong navy.

Which action resulted from the building of the Panama Canal?
USH_U3_FSQ1_FY23
- Tools.
Panama's future vote on statehood
continuous warfare throughout Panama
Panama's political freedom from Colombia
attacks on Panama by Central American rebels

The building of the Panama Canal resulted in Panama's political freedom from Colombia, as the United States supported Panama's independence movement and helped secure its independence, leading to the construction of the canal.

Which United States document contained the Teller Amendment guaranteeing Cuban independence?
о
Treaty of Paris (1898)
Treaty of Versailles (1919)
congressional resolution for war against Germany (1917)
congressional resolution for military action against Spain (1898)
The Teller Amendment guaranteeing Cuban independence was included in the congressional resolution for military action against Spain in 1898.

Why did President Harry Truman create the Loyalty Review Program in 1947?
O The government hoped to ease the economic problems in Europe.
• The government feared the intrusion of communists into American politics
O The government grew concerned about an attack by the Japanese military.
• The government sought to ease the transition of military men and women returning to domestic life,
President Harry Truman created the Loyalty Review Program in 1947 because the government feared the intrusion of communists into American politics.

What was a result of actions taken by the House Un-American Activities Committee (HUAC)?
r Tools
O
Blacklisted Americans were fired from jobs.
Americans who supported Communism were deported.
Soldiers suspected of favoring communism were forced to leave the military.
Communist supporters attempted a violent takeover of the federal government.

A result of actions taken by the House Un-American Activities Committee (HUAC) was that blacklisted Americans were fired from jobs, as the committee investigated allegations of communist influence in US government, Hollywood, and other areas, leading to blacklists that ruined many careers.

Much of the evidence now available of the illegal actions of Communists... is derived from wiretapping surveillance... Wiretap evidence is now inadmissible in prosecutions in Federal courts... [It is essential that the information we procure be admissible in court. . .. [Thus, we call for] the enactment of legislation to authorize the introduction in Federal courts, in national security cases, of evidence procured by wire-tapping.
-U.S. Attorney General Herbert Brownell, 1954
Source: U.S. Department of Justice/Public Domain
How did the attitude expressed in this excerpt impact the cultural climate of the early 1950s?
A political culture developed in support of increased domestic spying.
A popular culture developed that expressed fear of a foreign influence.
A counterculture developed that sought to create an alternative to mainstream society.
A youth culture developed on college campuses that called for an expansion of civil rights.
The attitude expressed in this excerpt impacted the cultural climate of the early 1950s by creating a political culture that supported increased domestic spying and government surveillance.

Which government action was taken as a result of the Red Scare during the 1950s?
President Eisenhower increased the size of the armed forces.
O Several U.S. Supreme Court justices were removed from office.
• President Eisenhower encouraged the adoption of isolationist policies.
The House Un-American Activities Committee conducted investigations.

The House Un-American Activities Committee conducted investigations as a result of the Red Scare during the 1950s, which investigated allegations of communist influence in US government, Hollywood and other areas, leading to blacklists that ruined many careers.

Which phrase best describes the primary goal of the Marshall Plan?
to enable a return to isolationism
O
to prevent a conflict between allies
to encourage the regulation of banks
to prevent the spread of communism
The primary goal of the Marshall Plan was to prevent the spread of communism by providing financial aid to Europe after World War II and promoting economic recovery and stability.

How did the Truman Doctrine compare with the Marshall Plan?
Both sought to restore political stability, but the primary focus of the Marshall Plan was economic recovery.
O Both sought the containment of Communism, but the primary focus of the Marshall Plan was to reunite Germany.
O
Both were effects of the start of the Korean War, but the primary focus of the Truman Doctrine was to occupy Japan.
Both were effects of the end of World War II, but the primary focus of the Truman Doctrine was to identify domestic Communists.

Both the Truman Doctrine and the Marshall Plan sought the containment of Communism, but the primary focus of the Marshall Plan was economic recovery through financial aid to Europe after World War II, while the Truman Doctrine provided military and economic assistance to countries threatened by communism, mainly in Greece and Turkey.

How did the Soviet Union react when West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)?
The Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact.
The Soviet Union wrote the Kellogg-Briand Pact.
The Soviet Union signed the Paris Peace Accords.
The Soviet Union negotiated the Camp David Accords.
When West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), the Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact, a military alliance in response to the potential threat from NATO.

How did the Soviet Union react when West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)?
The Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact.
The Soviet Union wrote the Kellogg-Briand Pact.
The Soviet Union signed the Paris Peace Accords.
The Soviet Union negotiated the Camp David Accords.
When West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), the Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact, a military alliance in response to the potential threat from NATO.

How did the Soviet Union react when West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO)?
The Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact.
The Soviet Union wrote the Kellogg-Briand Pact.
The Soviet Union signed the Paris Peace Accords.
The Soviet Union negotiated the Camp David Accords.
When West Germany became a member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), the Soviet Union established the Warsaw Pact, a military alliance in response to the potential threat from NATO.

How did nuclear technology impact the economy of the United States after World War 11?
-R Tool
It became the most affordable source of power.
O
It became a major focus of military spending.
It became an important export commodity.
It became a major source of tax revenue.
Nuclear technology became a major focus of military spending and development of atomic weapons after World War II which had a significant impact on the economy of the United States.

Which issue had the greatest effect on the proliferation of nuclear weapons during President Dwight D. Eisenhower's administration?
the elimination of test-ban treaties
A
the establishment of the domino theory
the adoption of the massive retaliation doctrine
the development of the military-industrial complex

The adoption of the massive retaliation doctrine had the greatest effect on the proliferation of nuclear weapons during President Dwight D. Eisenhower's administration, as it prioritized the development of a nuclear arsenal to deter potential enemies from attacking the United States or its allies, leading to an arms race between the US and the Soviet Union.

How did the development and use of nuclear technology during World War II affect future relationships?
34 Tools
Nuclear weapons were used at the Battle of Stalingrad and caused political rivalries between the Soviet Union and United States.
Nucled weapons were used at the Battle of Midway and caused political tension in Southeast Asia and Japan.
The bombing of Dresden caused countries to consider the economic implications of using nuclear power.
The bombing of Hiroshima caused countries to consider the social implications of using nuclear power.

The bombing of Hiroshima caused countries to consider the social and political implications of using nuclear power in future conflicts and international relations, leading to arms races and a fear of nuclear war during the Cold War era.

What was the primary objective of the United States' military involvement in Korea during the 1950s?
A Tools
to promote economic interests
to contain communist aggression
to occupy a former enemy territory
to honor an international trade agreement

The primary objective of the United States' military involvement in Korea during the 1950s was to contain communist aggression, as North Korea had invaded South Korea, prompting intervention by the UN, including the US military.

Which statement describes the significance of the 38th parallel during the Korean War?
Tools
It represents the farthest advance by the North Korean army.
It identifies the location of the first South Korean military victory.
It identifies the general location of the Korean demilitarized zone.
It represents the border between the Korean peninsula and China.
The 38th parallel represents the general location of the Korean demilitarized zone and was a key dividing line between North and South Korea during the Korean War.

Which result was a consequence of the Korean War?
The country was divided into North and South Korea.
A
The United States was forced to surrender territory.
O
Cold War policies were established in the region.
Communism spread from Korea into China.

The country was divided into North and South Korea was a consequence of the Korean War, as the conflict ended in an armistice rather than a peace treaty, leading to the lasting division of Korea into two separate nations.

Which term best describes the U.S. reaction to the launch of Sputnik?
nuclear proliferation
O
containment policy
domino theory
space race

The term "space race" best describes the U.S. reaction to the launch of Sputnik, as it prompted a competition between the US and the Soviet Union to achieve milestones in space exploration and technology, including the landing on the moon.

Which term best describes the U.S. reaction to the launch of Sputnik?
nuclear proliferation
containment policy
domino theory
space race

The term "space race" best describes the U.S. reaction to the launch of Sputnik, as it prompted a competition between the US and the Soviet Union to achieve milestones in space exploration and technology, including the landing on the moon.
which term best describes it?
The best term to describe the US reaction to the launch of Sputnik is "space race."

World News
*
January 1959
Castro Takes Control! Which action was a result of the event in the headline?
The U.S. government signed a treaty with the new leadership.
The United States accepted thousands of Cuban immigrants.
The U.S. government instituted a military draft.
The United States declared war on Cuba.
A result of Castro taking control of Cuba as indicated in the headline was that the United States accepted thousands of Cuban immigrants, as many people fled Cuba seeking asylum in the US.

Read the following list about the middle class in the United States between 1950 and 1960 and answer the question below.
• After World War II, the United States experienced a boom in population.
•Between 1950 and 1960, fourteen of the largest fifteen cities declined in population.
• Congress spent billions of dollars on the development of highways. growth of suburbs
reduction in automobile prices
reduction of public-housing projects
expansion of rural-electrification programs

Which factor contributed most to the growth of suburbs in the United States between 1950 and 1960?
Congress spending billions of dollars on the development of highways contributed the most to the growth of suburbs in the United States between 1950 and 1960, as it allowed people to live farther away from city centers and easily commute to work or other areas.
What was the result of the changes described by this list?

The changes described by the list resulted in the growth of suburbs, which had a significant impact on American society, transforming the population distribution, urban planning, and the economy, as they became centers of retail, entertainment, and employment.

REGISTERED BIRTHS IN THE UNITED STATES, 1940-1960
1960
1940
1950
2,360,399
3,554,149
4,257,850
Source: U.S. Census Bureau
Which reason best explains this change in the registered births?
• prosperity following a conflict
• immigration due to demobilization
• regulations ensuring healthful diets
• immunizations preventing childhood diseases

The reason that best explains this change in the registered births in the United States from 1940 to 1960 is prosperity, as there was significant economic growth during this time period that boosted population and living standards.

the population had graduated from a school of higher learning. By 1999, the figure was 25 percent. attributable to increased education.

A substantial part of the growth of the economy is
Which development most contributed to the educational and economic trends discussed in the excerpt?
O Civil Rights Movement
• growth of suburbs
O
baby boom
GI Bill

The GI Bill most contributed to the educational and economic trends discussed in the excerpt, as it provided financial assistance to millions of World War II veterans to attend college or vocational schools, leading to an increase in educational attainment and more opportunities in the labor market.

Which idea best characterizes the Beat Generation during the 1950s?
restoring fundamental moral values to society
O
promoting nonconformity and anti-consumerism
O
leading international efforts to contain communism
O
supporting goals of big business and personal prosperity

The idea that best characterizes the Beat Generation during the 1950s was promoting nonconformity and anti-consumerism, as it rejected mainstream culture and conformity, and promoted a more free, individualistic, and artistic lifestyle that criticized the consumerism, materialism, and social conventions of the era.

I believe that it must be the policy of the United States to support free peoples who are resisting attempted subjugation by armed minorities or by outside pressures.
President Harry Tuman, from a 1947 speech
Source: National Archives of the United Siste
Which phrase describes the context for this speech?
forming a military alliance with Western European nations
providing resources for creating a democratic society in Japan adopting a policy for containing the influence of the Soviet Union
implementing a free trade agreement with Latin American nations

The phrase that describes the context for this speech is "adopting a policy for containing the influence of the Soviet Union," as President Truman was announcing what would be known as the Truman Doctrine, which provided military and economic assistance to countries threatened
